item.title: Putin and Zelenskiy announce rival ceasefires for Victory Day commemorations
item.description: President Putin declared a two-day ceasefire on May 8-9 to mark Russia WWII Victory Day, while Zelenskiy countered with an earlier proposed ceasefire starting May 5-6. Putin warned of massive missile strikes on Kyiv if Victory Day celebrations are disrupted.

item.title: Australia raises rates for third time this year as oil shock bites
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia lifted rates to 4.35%, warning inflation would remain sticky as the Middle East conflict unleashed a global oil shock. Headline inflation is now seen peaking near 5%, with growth downgrades expected.

item.title: US prepared for visa sanctions on China over migrant repatriation slowdown
item.description: A senior Trump administration official warned that China is slowing efforts to repatriate Chinese nationals in the US illegally. Washington is prepared to increase travel restrictions if Beijing does not reverse course.

item.title: Indian rupee hits record low as US-Iran tensions rattle markets
item.description: The Indian rupee slid to a record low of 95.40 against the dollar after US-Iranian strikes in the Gulf rattled markets. The fall deepens concerns for the oil-importing economy amid fading hopes for a resolution.

item.title: German foreign minister confident on NATO deterrence despite US troop cuts
item.description: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul expressed confidence there would be no shortfall in NATO conventional deterrence capability in Europe after Washington announced troop drawdowns in Germany.

item.title: Apple in Talks with Intel and Samsung for US Chip Production
item.description: Apple has held exploratory discussions about using Intel and Samsung Electronics to produce main processors for its devices in the United States, as part of efforts to diversify its chip supply chain.

item.title: Trump Calls on South Korea to Join Hormuz Protection Mission
item.description: President Trump said South Korea should join US efforts to protect ships near Iran, with Defense Secretary Hegseth scheduled to hold a news conference with the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.

item.title: Russian Court Freezes Assets of Rusagro Billionaire Founder
item.description: A Russian court has frozen the assets of Vadim Moshkovich, billionaire founder of agricultural producer Rusagro, in what could be another high-profile asset grab by the state.

item.title: Trump Family Crypto Venture Countersues Justin Sun for Defamation
item.description: World Liberty Financial, the crypto venture co-founded by President Trump and his sons, filed a defamation lawsuit against Hong Kong-based crypto entrepreneur Justin Sun, escalating a dispute with one of its most prominent backers.

item.title: Gulf Markets Rise on Hopes of Middle East Resolution
item.description: Most Gulf stock exchanges closed slightly higher, supported by limited signs that the Middle East conflict may be resolved.

item.title: India condemns Fujairah attack after three nationals injured in Iranian drone strike
item.description: India expressed strong condemnation after three Indian citizens were injured in an Iranian drone attack on the UAE port of Fujairah, calling the incident "unacceptable" and urging peaceful resolution of the ongoing conflict.

item.title: Argentina Jewish community targeted in Molotov cocktail attacks near Buenos Aires
item.description: A Jewish library and Chabad center in a suburb near Buenos Aires were attacked with Molotov cocktails, with local counterterrorism unit investigating the incident. No injuries were reported.

item.title: Pakistan foils suicide attack near Afghan border, one civilian killed
item.description: Pakistani security forces thwarted a suicide attack after an attacker attempted to ram an explosives-laden vehicle into a military post in South Waziristan, resulting in one civilian death.

item.title: Brazilian President Lula to meet Trump in US this week
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva will travel to the US to meet with President Trump, with hopes of strengthening the "good chemistry" between the two leaders for mutual benefit.

item.title: US Forces Battle Iran for Control of Strait of Hormuz as UAE Comes Under Attack
item.description: The US military fired on Iranian forces and sank six boats while attempting to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The UAE reported 15 missiles and 4 drones intercepted, with a drone sparking a fire at a key oil facility that wounded three Indian nationals. The fragile ceasefire from early April is under severe strain.

item.title: German Carmakers Slump as Trump Hikes Auto Tariffs to 25 Percent
item.description: Shares in German automakers fell sharply after President Trump raised tariffs on imported European cars from 15% to 25%, breaking from the previously agreed terms. The move deals a fresh blow to the already battered sector as transatlantic tensions escalate.

item.title: Japanese Yen Surges as Markets Brace for Potential Currency Intervention
item.description: The yen jumped sharply on Monday, sparking speculation that Japan had again intervened to support its sinking currency. Finance Minister Katayama warned against speculative moves in foreign exchange as pressure persists despite intervention risks.

item.title: Brazil Revives Consumer Debt Relief Program Ahead of October Election
item.description: Brazil relaunched its consumer debt relief program first introduced in 2023, aiming to cut interest burdens and boost disposable income ahead of President Lula da Silvas re-election bid in October. The move targets economic relief for struggling households.

item.title: Oman reports two injured as residential building in Bukha targeted amid Hormuz tensions
item.description: A residential building in Oman's Bukha region along the Strait of Hormuz coastline was targeted, injuring two people, as regional tensions escalate following US-Iran clashes in the strategic waterway.

item.title: Merz adopts conciliatory strategy to navigate Trump's anger over trade and security
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz is falling back on the familiar tactic of telling Trump he's right after a perilous blowup with Washington over trade and security tensions.

item.title: Nvidia CEO says China should not get most advanced chips but urges continued US sales
item.description: Jensen Huang said China should not have the company's most advanced chips but urged Washington to allow U.S. firms to continue selling to the Chinese market, highlighting the tech Cold War dilemma.

item.title: OCBC acquires HSBC's retail and wealth management operations in Indonesia
item.description: OCBC, Southeast Asia's second-largest banking group, agreed to acquire HSBC's retail and wealth management operations in Indonesia, marking a significant consolidation in regional banking.

item.title: Yen steady, dollar firms as Middle East war fuels safe-haven demand
item.description: The Japanese yen held steady on Tuesday after suspected intervention, while the US dollar gained on safe-haven flows as the US-Iran conflict kept markets on edge. Oil prices remain above $100 per barrel.

item.title: Trump's Germany troop cuts expose limits of NATO efforts to keep US on board
item.description: European officials scrambling to keep the US in NATO face a setback as Trump abruptly orders force reductions in Germany, a move that comes amid tensions over the Iran war and questions about US commitment to European security.

item.title: Japan finance minister warns against FX speculation after yen jolt
item.description: Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama issued a warning against speculative currency moves after the yen's sudden spike on Monday triggered speculation that Tokyo had intervened to support its weakening currency.
